title: Notice of Privacy Practices
description: Provide patients with Notice of Privacy Practices at first encounter and post prominently per Privacy Rule.

title: Minimum Necessary Standard
description: Limit PHI use, disclosure, and request to the minimum necessary to accomplish the intended purpose.

title: Patient Right to Access PHI
description: Provide patient access to PHI within 30 days of request with one 30-day extension permitted.

title: Patient Right to Amendment
description: Allow patients to request amendments to their PHI and respond within 60 days.

title: Accounting of Disclosures
description: Provide accounting of PHI disclosures upon request covering up to six years prior.

title: Restriction Requests
description: Honor patient requests to restrict disclosures to health plans for fully-paid out-of-pocket services.

title: Confidential Communications
description: Accommodate reasonable patient requests for confidential communication channels.

title: Administrative Safeguards Risk Analysis
description: Conduct accurate and thorough risk analysis of PHI confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

title: Security Awareness Training
description: Implement security awareness and training program for all workforce members.

title: Workforce Sanctions Policy
description: Apply appropriate sanctions against workforce members who fail to comply with security policies.

title: Information System Activity Review
description: Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity such as audit logs.

title: Access Authorization
description: Implement policies and procedures for granting access to ePHI based on workforce role.

title: Unique User Identification
description: Assign unique name or number for identifying and tracking user identity in systems with ePHI.

title: Automatic Logoff
description: Implement electronic procedures that terminate session after predetermined period of inactivity.

title: Encryption and Decryption
description: Implement mechanism to encrypt and decrypt ePHI per addressable specification.

title: Audit Controls
description: Implement hardware, software, and procedural mechanisms to record and examine activity in systems containing ePHI.

title: Integrity Controls
description: Implement policies and procedures to protect ePHI from improper alteration or destruction.

title: Person or Entity Authentication
description: Verify person or entity seeking access to ePHI is the one claimed.

title: Transmission Security
description: Implement technical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to ePHI transmitted over network.

title: Breach Notification to Patients
description: Notify affected individuals of breach within 60 days of discovery.

title: Breach Notification to HHS
description: Notify HHS of breach affecting 500+ individuals without unreasonable delay and within 60 days.

title: Breach Notification to Media
description: Notify prominent media outlets when breach affects 500+ residents of a state or jurisdiction.

title: Annual HHS Log Submission
description: Submit annual log to HHS of breaches affecting fewer than 500 individuals.

title: Business Associate Agreements
description: Execute BAA with all business associates accessing PHI before disclosure.

title: Business Associate Compliance
description: Hold business associates directly liable for HITECH-amended HIPAA Security and Privacy Rules.

title: Workstation Security
description: Implement physical safeguards for all workstations accessing ePHI.

title: Device and Media Controls
description: Track movement of hardware and electronic media containing ePHI.

title: Media Disposal
description: Implement procedures for final disposition of ePHI and hardware containing ePHI.

title: Media Re-Use
description: Implement procedures for removal of ePHI from electronic media before re-use.

title: Six-Year Documentation Retention
description: Maintain HIPAA-related documentation for at least six years from creation or last effective date.

title: HITRUST CSF Validated Assessment
description: Maintain HITRUST CSF Validated Assessment with annual interim review and biennial recertification.

title: HITRUST Information Protection Program
description: Document Information Protection Program with executive sponsorship and CISO accountability.

title: HITRUST Risk Treatment Plan
description: Maintain risk treatment plan with prioritized remediation roadmap for identified risks.

title: Electronic Records Validation
description: Validate systems generating electronic records used in FDA-regulated processes.

title: Electronic Signature Components
description: Use unique combinations of identification code and password for electronic signatures.

title: Computer-Generated Audit Trail
description: Use secure computer-generated time-stamped audit trails recording date, time, and operator of changes.

title: Role-Based Access Limitation
description: Limit system access to authorized individuals with role-based permissions.

title: Persons Performing Tasks Training
description: Document training for persons developing, maintaining, or using electronic record/signature systems.

title: Special Category Data Processing
description: Establish Article 9 condition for processing health data in addition to lawful basis under Article 6.

title: Right of Access for Health Data
description: Respond to data subject access requests for health records within one month.

title: 72-Hour Health Data Breach Notification
description: Notify supervisory authority of health data breach within 72 hours of awareness.

title: Explicit Consent for Health Data
description: Obtain explicit consent for processing health data unless another Article 9 condition applies.

title: DPIA for Large-Scale Health Processing
description: Conduct Data Protection Impact Assessment for large-scale processing of health data.

title: DPO Required for Health Processing
description: Appoint Data Protection Officer when core activities involve large-scale health data processing.

title: Health Data Transfer Safeguards
description: Use appropriate safeguards for transfers of health data outside EEA.

title: Common Criteria for Healthcare
description: Implement AICPA TSC Common Criteria including security, availability, and confidentiality.

title: PHI Access Continuous Monitoring
description: Continuously monitor PHI access with automated alerting on anomalous patterns.

title: Healthcare Vendor Risk Assessment
description: Risk-assess vendors handling PHI before onboarding with annual review.

title: Healthcare-Aware ISMS
description: Tailor ISMS scope and risk assessment to healthcare-specific information assets.

title: PHI Encryption Standards
description: Apply ISO 27001 cryptographic controls to PHI at rest and in transit.

title: Medical Device QMS
description: Implement quality management system per ISO 13485 for medical device software.

title: Software Lifecycle Management
description: Establish documented software development lifecycle compliant with IEC 62304.

title: Medical Device Risk Management
description: Apply ISO 14971 risk management throughout medical device software lifecycle.

title: 510(k) Premarket Notification
description: Submit 510(k) for Class II medical device software substantially equivalent to predicate.

title: De Novo Classification Request
description: Submit De Novo classification for novel low-to-moderate risk medical device software.

title: Medical Device Reporting
description: Report device-related deaths, serious injuries, and malfunctions to FDA via MDR.

title: Software as a Medical Device
description: Classify SaMD per IMDRF risk framework based on healthcare situation and significance.

title: Premarket Cybersecurity
description: Address premarket cybersecurity per FDA premarket cybersecurity guidance.

title: EPCS Compliance
description: Comply with DEA Electronic Prescriptions for Controlled Substances requirements including identity proofing.

title: DEA Two-Factor Authentication
description: Use DEA-required two-factor authentication for EPCS prescriber signing.

title: FHIR R4 Patient Access API
description: Provide standardized FHIR R4 patient access API per ONC Cures Act Final Rule.

title: SMART on FHIR Authorization
description: Implement SMART App Launch Framework with OAuth 2.0 for FHIR resource access.

title: USCDI Data Element Support
description: Support United States Core Data for Interoperability data classes in certified Health IT.

title: Information Blocking Prohibition
description: Avoid practices that interfere with electronic health information access, exchange, or use.

title: CMS Promoting Interoperability Program
description: Meet CMS Promoting Interoperability requirements for hospitals and providers.

title: MIPS Quality Reporting
description: Submit Merit-based Incentive Payment System quality measures via certified Health IT.

title: Genetic Information Non-Discrimination
description: Protect genetic information per Genetic Information Nondiscrimination Act.

title: Substance Use Disorder Records
description: Apply 42 CFR Part 2 enhanced consent requirements for substance use disorder records.

title: EU MDR Class IIa+ Compliance
description: Comply with EU Medical Device Regulation for Class IIa+ medical device software.

title: UDI Carrier on Medical Device
description: Apply Unique Device Identification carrier per EU MDR Annex VI.

title: IVDR Compliance
description: Comply with EU In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation for IVD software.

title: GxP System Validation
description: Validate computerized systems supporting GxP processes per GAMP 5 framework.

title: Audit Trail Review
description: Review electronic audit trails for GxP-relevant data integrity issues.

title: ALCOA+ Data Integrity
description: Maintain ALCOA+ data integrity (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate).

title: Telehealth PHI Protection
description: Apply HIPAA Security Rule to telehealth platforms transmitting and storing PHI.

title: Joint Commission IM Standards
description: Meet Joint Commission Information Management standards for accredited organizations.

title: EMTALA Anti-Dumping
description: Comply with EMTALA medical screening and stabilization requirements.

title: Stark Law Self-Referral Prohibition
description: Avoid prohibited physician self-referrals to entities with financial relationships.

title: Anti-Kickback Statute
description: Avoid soliciting, receiving, offering, or paying remuneration for Medicare/Medicaid referrals.

title: False Claims Act Compliance
description: Avoid submitting false or fraudulent claims to federal healthcare programs.

title: OIG Compliance Program Elements
description: Implement seven elements of effective compliance program per OIG guidance.

title: ICD-10-CM Coding Compliance
description: Use current ICD-10-CM codes per Official Guidelines for Coding and Reporting.

title: CPT Coding Accuracy
description: Apply CPT codes accurately based on service documentation per AMA guidelines.

title: Controlled Substance Inventory
description: Maintain biennial controlled substance inventory and retention records.

title: DSCSA Track and Trace
description: Implement Drug Supply Chain Security Act track-and-trace serialization.

title: FERPA Student Health Records
description: Apply FERPA protections to student health records held by educational agencies.

title: COPPA for Pediatric Apps
description: Obtain verifiable parental consent before collecting personal info from children under 13.

title: TCPA Patient Outreach
description: Comply with TCPA before automated patient outreach via SMS or auto-dialer.

title: CAN-SPAM for Healthcare Email
description: Comply with CAN-SPAM Act for commercial healthcare email communications.

title: ADA Web Accessibility
description: Meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility for patient-facing portals per ADA Title III.

title: Section 508 Accessibility
description: Meet Section 508 standards for federal healthcare technology procurement.

title: FDA AI/ML SaMD Action Plan
description: Address FDA Good Machine Learning Practice for AI/ML-based SaMD.

title: EU AI Act for Healthcare
description: Classify healthcare AI systems per EU AI Act risk categories with conformity assessment.

title: Research Authorization
description: Obtain HIPAA-compliant authorization or IRB waiver for PHI use in research.

title: Common Rule for Human Subjects
description: Comply with revised Common Rule for federally-funded human subjects research.

title: Scientific Research Exception
description: Apply GDPR Article 89 safeguards when processing health data for scientific research.

title: CLIA Laboratory Certification
description: Maintain Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments certification for lab software.

title: DICOM Conformance
description: Comply with DICOM standard for medical imaging interoperability.

title: HL7 v2 / FHIR Messaging
description: Use HL7 v2 or FHIR R4 standards for clinical messaging and data exchange.

title: California Health App Privacy
description: Apply CCPA rights to California consumers using health-related apps and services.
